commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Maintainers need to decide and record whether the intentional compatibility break for stale allowlist-only subagent ids is acceptable before merge.; Cleared: The diff narrows the subagent command execution surface and does not add dependencies, network calls, CI changes, secret handling, or package-resolution changes. (sha=ff00751f7d182315427b0ab40dad1139d90875d5)
commands.review_summary:
The PR filters `subagents.allowAgents` targets through configured agent registries for `agents_list` and `sessions_spawn`, adds doctor detection/repair for stale allowlist entries, and updates docs, tests, and changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main's target policy returns allowlist-only ids from explicit allowlists and mixed wildcard allowlists, and the linked issue gives concrete stale config steps. I did not run live reproduction because this was a read-only review, but the PR body includes before/after Crabbox proof.
commands.review_followup: Maintainers need to decide and record whether the intentional compatibility break for stale allowlist-only subagent ids is acceptable before merge.

target.title: fix: require configured subagent allowlist targets
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `subagents.allowAgents` could make stale, unconfigured agent ids appear spawnable.
- Solution: filter allowed subagent targets through the configured target registry before listing or spawning.
- What changed: `agents_list` omits stale allowlist-only ids, `sessions_spawn` rejects them with a configured-registry error, and `openclaw doctor --fix` can clean stale subagent allowlists.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no broad compatibility fallback for implicit default-inheriting stale ids.

## Motivation

- Closes #84811. The reported breakage was that deleting an agent config did not remove the id from the effective subagent target surface when it remained in `allowAgents`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #84811
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: stale `agents.list[].subagents.allowAgents` entries for deleted agents were still advertised and accepted as subagent targets.
- Real environment tested: direct AWS Crabbox Linux lease with live Gateway and OpenAI Responses model.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 180m --timing-json --env-from-profile /private/tmp/iao.env --allow-env OPENAI_API_KEY --script .artifacts/issue-84811/fix-proof.sh`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): direct AWS Crabbox `provider=aws`, run `run_9d964f152f77`, lease `cbx_1caae39655bc`, exit 0. Assertions: `agentsListCalled=true`, `sessionsSpawnCalled=true`, `listOmitsResearch=true`, `spawnForbidden=true`, `configuredRegistryError=true`, `noResearchChildSession=true`.
- Observed result after fix: `agents_list` returned no `research` entry; `sessions_spawn(agentId:"research")` returned `status:"forbidden"` with `agentId "research" is not in the configured agent registry (allowed: none)`; only main-session transcripts existed.
- What was not tested: non-OpenAI live providers; full suite.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): direct AWS Crabbox current-main repro previously showed `agents_list` included `research` as `configured:false` and `sessions_spawn` accepted `agent:research:subagent:*`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the subagent target policy treated explicit `allowAgents` entries as spawnable ids without intersecting them with configured agent targets.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression coverage for stale explicit allowlist ids after an agent config was removed.
- Contributing context (if known): `agents_list` and `sessions_spawn` shared the same permissive policy, so the stale target was both advertised and accepted.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/subagent-target-policy.test.ts`, `src/agents/tools/agents-list-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.allowlist.test.ts`, `src/commands/doctor/shared/stale-subagent-allowlist.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: stale allowlist-only ids are omitted from `agents_list`, rejected by `sessions_spawn`, and removable by doctor without breaking configured ACP targets.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers the pure policy, user-facing tools, and config repair boundary touched by the fix.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): adjacent ACP spawn tests cover configured ACP target preservation.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`allowAgents` entries must now point at configured subagent targets. Stale ids are no longer accepted as implicit default-inheriting agents; add a minimal `agents.list[]` entry to keep a target spawnable, or run `openclaw doctor --fix` to remove stale entries.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
allowAgents:["research"] + no agents.list research -> agents_list advertises research -> sessions_spawn accepts research

After:
allowAgents:["research"] + no agents.list research -> agents_list omits research -> sessions_spawn rejects research
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: the subagent tool surface is narrowed to configured targets, reducing accidental stale-target execution.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux on direct AWS Crabbox
- Runtime/container: Node 24 / repo checkout
- Model/provider: OpenAI Responses, `openai/gpt-5.5`
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway + agent CLI, no channel credential
- Relevant config (redacted): only `main` configured; `main.subagents.allowAgents=["research"]`; no `research` agent entry

### Steps

1. Start Gateway with the stale allowlist config.
2. Run a live agent turn that calls `agents_list`.
3. Force a `sessions_spawn` call to `agentId:"research"`.

### Expected

- `agents_list` omits `research`.
- `sessions_spawn` returns forbidden with a configured-registry error.
- No research child session is created.

### Actual

- Matched expected behavior in Crabbox run `run_9d964f152f77`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: focused Vitest for policy/tool/spawn/doctor paths; autoreview; direct AWS Crabbox live proof.
- Edge cases checked: wildcard allowlists, explicit configured ids, explicit stale ids, implicit `main`, configured ACP targets, doctor cleanup.
- What you did **not** verify: full suite; non-OpenAI live providers.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? No
- Config/env changes? Yes
- Migration needed? Yes
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: add a minimal `agents.list[]` entry for any intended target id, or run `openclaw doctor --fix` to remove stale `allowAgents` ids.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operators relying on stale allowlist-only ids as implicit agents will see those spawns rejected.
  - Mitigation: docs explain the supported minimal `agents.list[]` entry and doctor cleanup path; tests lock the intentional behavior.
target.author: joshavant
